intro: Thelypodiopsis howellii, also known as Howell's desert cress, is a perennial plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Thelypodiopsis howellii include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and other sulfur-containing compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory activities. In herbal medicine, Thelypodiopsis howellii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs in remedies for respiratory and immune system support.
